item.title: Issue #2565 created
item.description:
`fn:dateTime-record`

The result type of fn:parts-of-dateTime is fn:dateTime-record?, but the spec also says this:

All entries will be present, even when the value is an empty sequence.

Similarly, fn:build-dateTime, with a parameter $value as fn:dateTime-record states that:

Components that are present in $value but with an empty value are treated as if they were absent.

I think these are contradictions, given the current definition of fn:dateTime-record.

All of the components of an fn:dateTime-record may be absent, but they have an occurrence of exactly-one. Thus a map with an empty sequence as the value of some entry, as requested for the result of fn:parts-of-dateTime, cannot be an instance of fn:dateTime-record - it would violate the occurrence constraint.

The prose, along with test cases (e.g. the recently added fn-build-dateTime-gYear-from-map-with-empty-fields) suggest that the occurrence indicator for fn:dateTime-record components should possibly be zero-or-one.
